# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our application that needs to write logs and temporary files. Copy application files and set up directories for logs and cache. The container needs write permissions to these directories. Keep permissions flexible for ease of development and debugging.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:3.11-slim

# Create a non-root user with specific UID/GID
RUN groupadd --gid 1001 appgroup && \
    useradd --uid 1001 --gid appgroup --shell /bin/false --create-home appuser

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy dependency files first for better layer caching
COPY requirements.txt ./

# Install dependencies
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip && \
    pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy application files with appropriate ownership
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup . /app/

# Create directories for logs and cache with restricted permissions
# 750: owner (rwx), group (r-x), others (no access)
RUN mkdir -p /app/logs /app/cache /app/tmp && \
    chown -R appuser:appgroup /app/logs /app/cache /app/tmp && \
    chmod 750 /app/logs /app/cache /app/tmp

# Ensure application files are not world-writable
# 550 for executables/directories, 440 for regular files owned by appuser
RUN find /app -type f -not -path "/app/logs/*" -not -path "/app/cache/*" -not -path "/app/tmp/*" -exec chmod 640 {} + && \
    find /app -type d -not -path "/app/logs" -not -path "/app/cache" -not -path "/app/tmp" -exec chmod 750 {} +

# Set secure environment variables
# Prevent Python from writing .pyc files and enable unbuffered output for logging
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    LOG_DIR=/app/logs \
    CACHE_DIR=/app/cache \
    TMPDIR=/app/tmp

# Drop all unnecessary capabilities and switch to non-root user
USER appuser

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
    CMD ["python", "-c", "import sys; sys.exit(0)"]

# Use exec form to avoid shell injection and ensure proper signal handling
ENTRYPOINT ["python"]
CMD ["app.py"]
